figures.caption: FIG. 4. Absolute susceptibility χ|m|(T ) for phi4-mini:3.8b on even-L lattices (L = 10, 16, 20, 24). Shaded bands show ±1σ seed uncertainty. Peaks are sharp and centered at T ≈ 0.2, yielding γ/ν = 1.75 ± 0.13. The central value is statistically compatible with the 2D Ising exponent, but the vanishing effective coupling J̃ ≈ 0 (Fig. 7) rules out genuine Ising criticality; we interpret the proximity as a coincidence reflecting the steepness of the field crossover (see text).

FIG. 5. Absolute susceptibility χ|m|(T ) for llama3.1:8b on even-L lattices (L = 10, 16, 20, 24). Shaded bands show ±1σ seed uncertainty. The effective exponent γ/ν = 1.02 ± 0.05 corresponds to χmax ∼ L, a sub-linear growth indicating weak collective amplification.

FIG. 6. Absolute susceptibility χ|m|(T ) for mistral:7b on even-L lattices, up to L = 35. Peaks are very broad, centered around T ≈ 1.0–1.2, and grow as γ/ν = 2.01 ± 0.04, corresponding to trivial volume scaling χmax ∝ N (γ/ν are computed on even-L only). This reflects a field-dominated system whose fluctuations scale with size rather than with a diverging correlation length.
